dependency_discoveries.dependency: Proposed block produced by the consensus loop
Content-hash primitive for the block signature

dependency_discoveries.evidence: pi vocab search COMMIT → event only
pi vocab search HASH → no block hash (only tx hash + generic keccak)
pi vocab search BALANCE → event only, no capability

authoring_decisions.capability: Commit a proposed block to the canonical chain
authoring_decisions.rationale: Nothing commits a proposed block; only an unemitted block-committed event exists. This is the core gap the CR fills.
authoring_decisions.alternatives_checked: searched COMMIT — only blockchain::EV_BLOCK_COMMITTED_V0 (an event), no committing capability

authoring_decisions.capability: Store the canonical chain ledger and track its head
authoring_decisions.rationale: No append-only ledger store exists to hold committed blocks and the chain head.
authoring_decisions.alternatives_checked: only blockchain::STRUCTURE_BLOCKCHAIN_ORCHESTRATION_STORAGE_V0 exists — orchestration storage, not a chain store

authoring_decisions.capability: Validate a block's predecessor link before commit
authoring_decisions.rationale: Nothing enforces one predecessor per committed block against the chain head.
No block-canonical hash exists; a new pure transform canonicalises the block and hashes it, reusing the generic keccak primitive underneath.
authoring_decisions.alternatives_checked: capability_transforms::CT_PURE_KECCAK256_HASH_V0 (generic bytes) and blockchain::CC_HASH_TRANSACTION_V0 (transaction-specific) — neither hashes a block

authoring_decisions.capability: Bootstrap the genesis chain by creating the first block and initialising the supply
authoring_decisions.rationale: No genesis or bootstrap capability exists; a one-time workflow creates the genesis block and initialises the chain before consensus runs.
authoring_decisions.alternatives_checked: searched GENESIS and BOOTSTRAP — Matches (0) for both

authoring_decisions.rationale: Minting already exists under a mint policy and can serve the genesis supply without a new monetary mechanism.

authoring_decisions.capability: Announce that a block was committed
authoring_decisions.rationale: The block-committed event already exists; the new commit capability emits it (payload settled in analysis Q1, bound at design).

authoring_decisions.capability: Record a committed block using the existing block schema
authoring_decisions.rationale: The block entity already defines the record the chain commits.
The human decided balances are derived and reconciled on-chain post-commit; no reconciliation capability exists to realise it.
authoring_decisions.alternatives_checked: searched BALANCE — only blockchain::EV_BALANCE_RECONCILED_V0 (an event), no reconciliation capability
authoring_decisions.source_finding: S1 known_facts #11; S2 business_processes Reconcile wallet balances after commit

authoring_decisions.rationale: The balance-reconciled event already exists; the reconciliation capability emits it.

placement_decision.rationale: The canonical ledger is a distinct governance concern from block proposal (consensus) and from the adjacent wallet, transaction, mempool, and identity subdomains; the human declared it a created boundary and no existing subdomain owns committed history.
placement_decision.source_finding: S1 cr_type NEW_SUBDOMAIN; S1 governance_scope chain CREATED

saturation.evidence: every HIGH gap (commit, chain store, genesis bootstrap) has a committed AUTHOR_NEW decision; the MEDIUM predecessor-validation gap likewise

saturation.criterion: No dependency expansion in the last pass
saturation.evidence: the dependency set closed to reuse-or-author-new; no new INVESTIGATE dependencies surfaced

saturation.criterion: Verification pass complete, no OVERTURNED item unresolved
Every INFERRED finding promoted, accepted, or carried with a reason
saturation.evidence: the single INFERRED finding (Q1 event payload) is accepted at analysis level; exact field schema explicitly carried to design (S6b)
